---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

base de datos

```{r}
setwd("C:/Users/edith clemente arena/Desktop/EPIFITAS -GRADIENTE/treeline-epiphytes/treeline-epiphytes/Doble_filtro/DATA")

#----------------------------------------------------
# FOROFITOS CON EPIFITAS
#----------------------------------------------------

data <- read.csv(
  "estrucPOBa.csv",
  header = TRUE,
  sep = ";",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    morphospe = iconv(morphospe,
                      from = "",
                      to = "UTF-8",
                      sub = ""),
    
    morphospe = gsub("\u00A0", " ", morphospe),

    morphospe = str_squish(morphospe)
  )
sort(unique(data$morphospe))##revisar las especies

# data <- read.csv(
#   "./Doble_filtro/Data/estrucPOBa.csv",
#   header = TRUE,
#   sep = ";",
#   stringsAsFactors = FALSE
# )

hospepi <- data %>%
  dplyr::select(
    plot,
    elevation,
    forof,
    morphospe,
    family,
    subplot,
    dap,
    height
  )

#----------------------------------------------------
# ARBOLES
#----------------------------------------------------

trees <- read.csv(
  "treealtu.csv",
  header = TRUE,
  sep = ";",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)


# trees <- read.csv(
#   "./Doble_filtro/Data/treealtu.csv",
#   header = TRUE,
#   sep = ";",
#   stringsAsFactors = FALSE
# )

tree <- trees %>%
  dplyr::select(
    plot,
    elevation,
    tagtree,
    family,
    subplot,
    NC,
    DBH,
    H
  )

#----------------------------------------------------
# RENOMBRAR VARIABLES DE ARBOLES
#----------------------------------------------------

tree2 <- tree %>%
  rename(
    forof = tagtree,
    dap = DBH,
    height = H
  )

#----------------------------------------------------
# RESUMEN DE EPIFITAS POR FOROFITO
#----------------------------------------------------

epi_por_arbol <- hospepi %>%
  group_by(forof) %>%
  summarise(
    presencia_epifitas = 1,
    riqueza = n_distinct(morphospe),
    abundancia_total = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#----------------------------------------------------
# UNIR TABLAS
#----------------------------------------------------

arboles <- tree2 %>%
  left_join(
    epi_por_arbol,
    by = "forof"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    presencia_epifitas = ifelse(is.na(presencia_epifitas), 0, presencia_epifitas),
    riqueza = ifelse(is.na(riqueza), 0, riqueza),
    abundancia_total = ifelse(is.na(abundancia_total), 0, abundancia_total)
  )

#----------------------------------------------------
# VERIFICAR
#----------------------------------------------------

str(arboles)

table(arboles$presencia_epifitas)

table(arboles$plot, arboles$presencia_epifitas)

```

```{r}
# convertir a factores
arboles$elev_f <- factor(arboles$elevation)
arboles$subplot_f <- factor(arboles$subplot)


#modelo
m_rich <- glmer(riqueza ~ elev_f + (1|subplot_f),
                family = poisson, data = arboles)

# resultados
summary(m_rich)
Anova(m_rich)
windows()
check_model(m_rich)
#revisar overdispersion
check_overdispersion(m_rich)# overdispercion

# Solucionando la sobre dispersión
m_rich_nb <- glmmTMB(
  riqueza ~ elev_f + (1 | subplot_f),
  family = nbinom2,
  data = arboles
) # modelo final!

# Diferencia entre modelos (poisson vs nv)
AIC(m_rich, m_rich_nb)# si la diferencia entre valores de AIC es mayor a 10, es significativa 

# Resultados:
summary(m_rich_nb)
Anova(m_rich_nb)

# Revisando
windows()
check_overdispersion(m_rich_nb)# no sobredispersión
check_model(m_rich_nb)

#Extraer las comparaciones de emmeans como data frame
emm <- emmeans(m_rich_nb, ~ elev_f)
pairs_df <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey") %>%
  as.data.frame()
#data frame para ggplot
pairs_df <- pairs_df %>%
  separate(contrast, into = c("group1", "group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
  mutate(
    group1 = gsub("elev_f", "", group1),
    group2 = gsub("elev_f", "", group2),
    y.position = max(richness$richness) + 
                 seq(0.2, by = 0.2, length.out = n())
  )


#GRAFICO con asteriscos
colnames(pairs_df)
pairs_df <- pairs_df %>%
  mutate(
    p.signif = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE            ~ "ns"
    )
  )
#plot con correcciones visuales 
pairs_sig <- pairs_df %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.05)
pairs_sig <- pairs_sig %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = max(richness$richness, na.rm = TRUE) +
                 seq(2, by = 2, length.out = n())
  )
riq_el <- ggplot(richness, aes(elev_f, richness)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.8) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pairs_sig,
    label = "p.signif",
    tip.length = 0.01
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "crossbar",
    width = 0.6,
    colour = "red",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  ylab("Epiphyte species richness per tree") +
  xlab("Elevation (m)")

# plot
riq_el

```

Abundancia

```{r}
# Negative binomial type 2: variance increases quadratically with mean
m_nb2 <- glmmTMB(
  abundancia_total ~ elev_f + (1 | subplot_f),
  family = nbinom2,
  data = arboles
)
Anova(m_nb2)
# Negative binomial type 2 with dispersion varying among elevations
# This is useful when some elevations are much more variable than others
m_nb2_disp <- glmmTMB(
  abundancia_total ~ elev_f + (1 | subplot_f),
  dispformula = ~ elev_f,
  family = nbinom2,
  data = arboles
)
Anova(m_nb2_disp)
# Zero-inflated negative binomial type 2
# Use only if DHARMa suggests excess zeros
m_zinb2 <- glmmTMB(
  abundancia_total ~ elev_f + (1 | subplot_f),
  ziformula = ~ 1,
  family = nbinom2,
  data = arboles
)
Anova(m_zinb2)
# Negative binomial without random effect
# Useful because your subplot variance is basically zero
m_nb2_noRE <- glmmTMB(
  abundancia_total ~ elev_f,
  family = nbinom2,
  data = arboles
)
Anova(m_nb2_noRE)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Compare candidate models
# ------------------------------------------------------------

AIC(
  m_nb2,
  m_nb2_disp,
  m_zinb2,
  m_nb2_noRE
)


# Replace this with the model with best AIC
best_model <- m_nb2_disp

sim_best <- simulateResiduals(best_model, n = 1000)

plot(sim_best)
testDispersion(sim_best)
testZeroInflation(sim_best)
testOutliers(sim_best)

check_overdispersion(best_model)


# Your DHARMa result is important:
# 
# testZeroInflation(sim_best)
# ratioObsSim = 0, p-value = 0.002
# 
# This does not mean zero inflation. It means the opposite: your model simulates zeros, but your data have no zeros or fewer zeros than expected. So the problem is probably that your abundance data are zero-truncated.
# 
# In other words, if your dataset only includes trees with at least one individual/record, then a normal negative binomial is not ideal. Use a zero-truncated negative binomial.




# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Check whether abundance has zeros
# ------------------------------------------------------------

table(abund$abundance == 0)
min(abund$abundance)

# If min(abundance) > 0, use a zero-truncated model.
# This is likely your case.

abund <- abund %>%
  mutate(
    elev_f = factor(elev_f),
    subplot_f = factor(subplot_f)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Final model candidate: zero-truncated NB2
# with elevation-specific dispersion
# ------------------------------------------------------------

m_abund_final <- glmmTMB(
  abundancia_total ~ elev_f + (1 | subplot_f),
  dispformula = ~ elev_f,
  family = nbinom2,
  data = arboles
)

summary(m_abund_final)
Anova(m_abund_final)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Diagnostics
# ------------------------------------------------------------

sim_final <- DHARMa::simulateResiduals(m_abund_final, n = 1000)

plot(sim_final)

DHARMa::testDispersion(sim_final)
DHARMa::testZeroInflation(sim_final)
DHARMa::testOutliers(sim_final)

check_overdispersion(m_abund_final)

# --------------------------------------------------


# Comparisons:
AIC(m_nb2, m_abund_final)
summary(m_nb2)
summary(m_abund_final)

res_nb <- simulateResiduals(m_nb2)
res_final <- simulateResiduals(m_abund_final)

testDispersion(res_nb)
testDispersion(res_final)

testZeroInflation(res_nb)
testZeroInflation(res_final)

testOutliers(res_nb)
testOutliers(res_final)
```

ya no se pudo hacer el modelo de cero truncado con la base de datos completo y solo use el modelo binomial negativo
